title: SENIOR SERVICES - 211info
text: IRCO AFRICA HOUSE SENIOR SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides home delivered meals, naturalization assistance, homebound assistance, limited transportation assistance, case management, health and wellness activities, and focal point leadership for older adults and their caregivers. An Information and Assistance specialist is available to provide information and referrals. 709 NE 102nd Avenue Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Age 60 and older. Some services have income eligibility criteria. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Meals: Varies (503) 484-6371 . Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Program phone (503) 484-6371 Nelli Salvador [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information FEES: Varies by service. Sliding scale fee for Oregon Project Independence (OPI) services. Some services are free. DOCUMENTS: ID for most services. For Naturalization services, permanent residency (green card) is required. LANGUAGES: English, Arabic, Vietnamese, Russian, Ukrainian, Spanish, Nepali, Burmese, and a few African languages are spoken by staff. Phone interpretation available for other languages. AREA SERVED: Citizenship program: Clackamas, Multnomah, and Washington Counties OPI: 82nd to 162nd and from the Willamette River to the Columbia River SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Services: Case/Care Management * Older Adults Friendly Outreach Programs Home Delivered Meals Immigration/Naturalization Legal Services * Older Adults Specialized Information and Referral * Older Adult/Aging Issues Wellness Programs * Older Adults Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT AND FINANCE - ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - YOUTH ACADEMIC SUPPORT AND PARENT ENGAGEMENT HOUSING/SHELTER - EMERGENCY RENTAL ASSISTANCE HOUSING/SHELTER - RENT ASSISTANCE FOR FAMILIES WITH CHILDREN INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - AFRICAN LEADERSHIP DEVELOPMENT INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - CHILDREN'S PROGRAMS INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - SERVICES AGAINST EXPLOITATION INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - YOUTH MENTORING LAW ENFORCEMENT SERVICES - GANG PREVENTION AND INTERVENTION MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - FAMILY PROGRAMS UTILITIES - UTILITY ASSISTANCE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
